agent_analysis.hypothesis_rationale: Higher local nonprofit branch density indicates supportive local philanthropic ecosystems or agglomeration effects, boosting fundraising efficiency.
agent_analysis.prior_art_notes: Okten & Weisbrod (2000) discuss how local market demand and clustering can improve nonprofit efficiency.
agent_analysis.interpretation: A statistically significant positive correlation was found. Higher local branch density is associated with higher fundraising efficiency, indicating positive spillover or clustering effects that benefit fundraising returns.
